observations.recommendation:
1.5. We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to issue an Office Order
creating the Inventory Committee with adequate members, including at least
one member from each of the Accounting and Property Divisions/Units, and
temporarily relieve them of all their regular duties until the physical inventory
process is complete.

1.6. We further recommended that once created, the Inventory Committee:
1.6.1. Strictly follow the guidelines and procedures for conducting the physical
count of PPE. This includes recognizing PPE items found at the station
and disposing of items that are non-existent or missing. The Accountant
and Property Officer should reconcile their records based on the results
of the actual physical count and make any necessary adjustments;
1.6.2. Adopt a uniform numbering system for PPE property numbers and
require the Property Unit to update the property stickers according to
the prescribed format and include all the necessary information as
specified in Sections 5.6 and 5.7 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020; and
1.6.3. Prepare the PIP for approval by the Municipal Mayor and submit the
same to COA at least 10 calendar days before the scheduled start of
inventory activities.

2.6. We reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Accountant agreed to
verify, review, and analyze its depreciation schedule, taking into consideration
the provisions provided under PAG 4 and PAG 6 of IPSAS 17 to arrive at an
accurate and reliable carrying balance of the PPE, and thereafter prepare the
appropriate adjusting entries.

3.4. We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to create an Inventory
Committee to focus on the physical count of local roads and direct the Municipal
Engineer, Municipal Accountant, and Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with
the accounting and reporting guidelines on the local roads asset management
system pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.

3.5. We further recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to enjoin:
3.5.1. The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Treasurer with a complete description and segregation of road
components for road projects; and
3.5.2. The Municipal Accountant disclose the total road network system in the
Notes to FS.

4.4. We recommended and Management agreed that:
4.4.1. Municipal Mayor create the Inventory Committee comprised of
members who can be relieved of their regular duties to devote full time
to conducting the physical inventory taking until the same is completed;
4.4.2. Inventory Committee prepare and submit a copy of the inventory reports
to the Auditor to verify compliance with Section 124 of the NGAS
Manual for LGUs, Volume I;
4.4.3. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer maintain the supplies
ledger cards and stock cards, respectively; and
4.4.4. Municipal Accountant strictly follow the Perpetual Inventory Method for
recording purchases of supplies and materials as required in the NGAS
Manual for LGUs.

5.11. We recommended and the Municipal Agriculturist agreed to:
5.11.1. Conduct an inventory of breeding stocks as of the end of each year and
thereafter render a report including the fair market value of these items
to the Municipal Accountant, for proper recording in the books of the
LGU; and
5.11.2. Keep a systematic record of the Municipality’s breeding stocks,
reporting births, deaths, and disposal, and in case of death and
34
disposal, facilitate requests for relief of accountability in accordance
with COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992, to
appropriately drop the amount from the books.

5.12. We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to disclose the
composition of the Biological Assets account in the Notes to Financial Statements
in accordance with IPSAS 27 to provide relevant information that may affect the
fairness of the presentation of the financial statements.

1.19 We recommended that the LCE create an Inventory Committee to conduct an actual physical inventory count of the PPE and report the results thereof in the RPCPPE to be submitted to the Auditor and Accounting Unit not later than January 31 of each year. The RPCPPE shall be reconciled with the accounting records, and the necessary adjusting entries shall be prepared before the statement date.

1.20 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the records of the affected accounts, including the additional property acquired during the year, determine the correct date of acquisition of the property, and prepare the corresponding adjusting entries, if necessary, to take up the depreciation for the period in accordance with IPSAS 17.

1.21 We likewise recommended the Municipal Accountant to update the lapsing schedule, reconcile the same with the book balances, and come up with reliable and accurate PPE balances for a fair presentation of the financial statements.

2.8 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare and maintain the required GLs and SLs for every controlling account, as well as fill out the proper and correct information that each column suggests, so that accurate and reliable financial information can be produced.

2.9 We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to arrange the GL accounts according to the sequence appearing in the Chart of Accounts and observe the highest standards of honesty, objectivity, and consistency in the keeping of accounts in accordance with Section 111, Chapter 2 of P.D. No. 1445.

1.7 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to exhaust all available remedies to secure the documents pertaining to the animals as reported in the books, with sufficient documentation of the validation undertaken.

1.8 We further recommended and the Municipal Agriculturist agreed to verify the status of the animals, their conditions, and the present caretaker, through physical inspection, update the fair market values, and submit the corresponding report, which could also serve as the basis to support a possible request for relief, if warranted under the circumstances.

4.8 We recommended and the LDRRM Council agreed to ensure that the 70% budget allocation for disaster prevention, mitigation, and preparedness is optimally utilized to better prepare the Municipality for any calamities that may happen in the future.

4.9 We further recommended and the LDRRM Council agreed to ensure that the programs and projects included in its LDRRM Plan and AIP are realistic and attainable.

5.8 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the CY 2023 monthly bank reconciliation statements to the Auditor immediately and comply with the provisions of Section 74 of P.D. No. 1445 and Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011.

We recommended that Management issue demand letters to the borrowers in order to collect the receivables and to thereafter designate an employee to monitor payments, to prepare demand letters, and make personal follow-up on these debtors in order that the loans will be collected.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor: a) Order the incumbent Paymaster/Disbursing Officer to withhold any payments due the said accountable officers until their respective accountabilities are extinguished; b) Direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to desist from processing cash advances unless supported by: b.1) Copy of the payroll or a list of payees with their net payments, for CAs for salaries, wages, allowances and similar payments; b.2) Estimate of expenses, for Cas for petty operating expenses; and b.3) Budget for current operating expenses for the agency field activity; c) Undertake reforms in the treasury functions, in terms of personnel and procedures, to ensure that disbursements are always based on approved vouchers, cash advances are regularly liquidated, collections are remitted daily and intact and the coffers of the government are of utmost priority; and, d) Direct the Municipal Accountant to monitor closely the timely submission of accounts from the Treasurer’s Office and to update regularly their records so that any discrepancies noted can be addressed immediately. e) We further recommended that a written report on the status of the foregoing accountabilities and the actions taken thereon be submitted to our office for further evaluation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:

a) create an Inventory Committee that will conduct the inventory taking full-
time until its completion pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 and
require the periodic physical inventory of supplies and properties as
provided in Section 124, Volume I of the MNGAS.

b) direct the Accounting and General Services Offices to maintain and keep
the necessary ledger/stock cards for all government properties and
conduct a regular reconciliation of their records as required by
regulations; and

c) require the Inventory Committee to submit to the audit team the RPCPPE,
RPCLRN and RPCI pursuant to existing rules and regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant to verify its records, review and analyze its depreciation schedule
taking into consideration the provisions provided under PAG 4 and PAG 6 of
IPSAS 17 to arrive at an accurate and reliable carrying balances of the PPE, and
thereafter prepare the appropriate adjusting entries.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain the
corresponding PPE Ledger Card for all property and equipment of the
municipality so that proper monitoring and accurate computation of depreciation
for each PPE can be generated.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to enforce the
collection of delinquent real property taxes by strictly implementing Sections 254
and 256 of RA No. 7160.

We further recommended that Management direct the Accounting and
Treasurer’s Offices to reconcile their records and account for the variance of
₱13,110,459.17 in tax delinquencies. We likewise recommended that Management
require the Accountant to prepare a correcting entry to take up the
understatement of ₱1,121,692.20 in RPT/SET Receivables for CY 2021, and to
ensure henceforth full compliance with the requirement of Section 20 of the
MNGAS.

We recommended and Management agreed to adhere strictly to the
provisions provide under the Negotiated Procurement as an alternative method
of procurement pursuant to the pertinent provisions of the 2016 Revised
Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184 for transparency,
effectiveness and efficiency in the procurement process.

We further recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC)
submit the other supporting documents with a written explanation on the noted
discrepancies mentioned above for review and evaluation by the audit team.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the treasurer to
prepare a Schedule of Unreleased Checks at the end of the year for submission to
the Accountant, who shall use this as basis in preparing a Journal Entry Voucher
(JEV) to restore the cash balance and recognize the liability, and another JEV at
the beginning of the following year to reverse the previous entry made. These
should form part of the supporting documents to the financial statements to be
submitted to the COA at year-end.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant to prepare and regularly maintain updated GLs and SLs with all the
necessary information provided therein under each column as required under
Section 2, Chapter 1 of the MNGAS.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit at the
end of the quarter the trial balance duly supported by schedules of subsidiary
ledger balances in accordance with Section 70 of the MNGAS, Volume I.

We recommended and Management agreed to (a) furnish the Auditor
copies of the contracts, purchase orders, and certificates of acceptance, together
with all supporting documents thereto, within the required timeframe; (b)
indicate all the basic information in the purchase order as required under COA
Circular Nos. 96-010 and 2009-001 to ensure delivery of items that meet the
specification.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer submit copies of the Certificate of Completion and Acceptance and the Municipal Accountant draw Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) to close the completed project by dropping the CIP account and recording the appropriate PPE accounts including prior years’ depreciation. Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer reconcile the ledgers and list of publicized projects at least quarterly in order that differences between the two records can be adjusted right away to ensure the accuracy and fairness of presentation of the CIP and related PPE accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 The Municipal Engineer had already submitted a Certificate of Completion upon every project’s 100% completion, while the Municipal Accountant recorded the related entries on the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct the required physical count of the inventory accounts in order to establish the existence and determine the reliability of the account balances shown in the financial statements at year-end. Likewise, we recommended the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant agreed to maintain the required stock cards and supplies ledger cards for each type of supplies, respectively, pursuant to Section 119 and 120 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting system for Local Government Units, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 Not Implemented as Inventory account, such as supplies, had no movement for quite a period of time while the physical inventory could not be traced.
observations.reason: Inventory account, such as supplies, had no movement for quite a period of time while the physical inventory could not be traced.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MSWD and the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer, as the case may be, submit the list bearing the names and corresponding signature of recipients of the food supplies procured and that the corresponding BAC submit the Omnibus Sworn Statement of each of the suppliers under the BGCM to the Office of the Auditor. We also recommended and the BAC/Municipal Health Officer/Municipal Accountant agreed to submit the Certificate of Product Registration and certification from the manufacturer that the supplier is an authorized distributor/dealer of the drugs and medicines purchased. We further recommended that the Municipal Engineer submit documents labeled 3.a-g above in compliance with Section 4(6) of P.D. No.1445 in order to ensure the validity, propriety and legality thereof. Lastly, we recommended that Management submit a written explanation why the posting requirements under Section 9 of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 were not complied with.

observations.reason: Other necessary documents had not yet been submitted to the Audit Team for verification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to see to it that two percent (2%) taxes on income were withheld from progress/final payments to service contractor pursuant to Section 2.57.2.J of the Revenue Regulations No. 11-2018, and likewise agreed to collect the deficient taxes from MAI Construction and Supply, otherwise the concerned officials shall be held personally liable therefor.

We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer issue an Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment for the One-Bagger Road Concrete Mixer and One-Concrete Assembly. Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw a journal entry to correct the Road Network account by recognizing Other Machineries and Equipment account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended AAR 2019 that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Engineer to immediately repair the comfort room of the rural health laboratory so that rural health office personnel can occupy/use it for its intended purpose.
observations.managementAction: The comfort room of the rural health laboratory were repaired and used by RHU personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended AAR 2019 that the Municipality fabricate or purchase colored waste bins to be distributed or set up in sets along public places and in populated areas, properly marked as “Compostable,” “Non-recyclable,” “Recyclable,” or “Special Wastes” or “Malata”, “Di Malata”, and “Di Mapuslan”. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require employees of the Environment and Natural Resources Office to implement and monitor compliance to the requirements of the law as to garbage segregation, collection and disposal in order to maintain sanitation and prevent the spread of diseases.
observations.managementAction: The LGU purchased 4 sets waste bins marked “Malata”,: Di Malata” and Di Mapuslan”. 2 were placed at the boulevard and 2 others were placed in the public market.

observations.recommendation: We recommended AAR 2018 that Management enroll casual employees in the GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG Fund and deduct from their wages the corresponding personal share in premiums in order that these employees will be entitled to the benefits thereof. We further recommended that the LGU ensure that the employer’s share in such premiums be appropriated in order that these employees will be entitled to the benefits. We also recommended that the LGU address similarly the omissions observed for PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG premiums.
observations.managementAction: The LGU appropriated funds for the employers share in AAR 2012 PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG Fund depending on their premiums, their performance. On the other hand, GSIS and Pag-ibig will return employees only if they contribute at least 3 years. Under this circumstances casual employees opted not to contribute, citing lack of security of tenure and may not recover the amount contributed to GSIS and Pag-ibig. All casual employees are PhilHealth and SSS members.
observations.reason: The plantilla of casual employees are renewed every 6 months by the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended AAR 2018 that management investigate the causes of the abnormal balances and effect the necessary correcting entries in order to fairly present the accounts in the financial statements as of December 31, 2018.
observations.managementAction: The accounting office conducted investigation and has an on-going investigation to determine the causes and effect the necessary correcting entries.
observations.reason: The accounting office still has an abnormal balance of ₱84,051.78 as of December 31, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended AAR 2018 that Management prioritize the procurement of early response and preparedness equipment and personnel training and to implement planned Mitigation programs and projects to ensure the availability of adequate facilities in case of disaster/calamity.
observations.managementAction: The unutilized quick response and mitigation funds were realigned to surge related COVID 19 expenses in CY 2020.
observations.reason: Utilization of Capital Expenditures MOOE- Mitigation were not given priority due to surge of COVID 19.

observations.recommendation: We recommended AAR 2018 that the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer and the Budget Officer ensure that only those projects/programs/activities which clearly address gender issues are allocated from GAD appropriations in order to ensure the attainment of GAD goals.
observations.managementAction: The programs and activities that mitigate the effect of COVID 19 were prioritized.
observations.reason: Most programs and activities that mitigate the effect of COVID 19 were prioritized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended AAR 2017 that the Office of the Municipal Accountant find all means to retrieve the records pertaining to the sources of the trust fund properties in order that the assets recorded in the trust fund can be totally transferred to the proper accounts in the general fund for proper presentation of the financial statements. Also, we recommended that the Offices of the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer maintain property, plant and equipment ledger cards and property cards, respectively, in accordance with existing regulations. Lastly, we recommended that the Municipality hire additional accounting personnel to augment the present manpower.
observations.managementAction: The accounting office partially acted to retrieve and transfer records and to maintain property, plant and equipment ledger cards and property cards. The Offices of the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer still did not maintain property, plant and equipment ledger cards and property cards respectively, in accordance with existing regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended AAR 2017 that the Municipal Mayor a) direct the Local Finance Committee to study and explore possibilities of increasing local revenues in order to minimize the municipality’s dependence on the IRA to finance its operations; b) direct the Municipal Treasurer to verify from the records of the BIR the accuracy of the declared gross receipts of the taxpayers in order to assess the correct amount of tax.
observations.managementAction: The LGU passed an amended Bayan Local Tax Code. The Sanggunian Panlalawigan was requested for review.
observations.reason: The amended Local Tax Code was submitted to the Sanggunian Panlalawigan for review.

observations.recommendation: We recommended AAR 2016 that the Municipal Mayor: a) Order the incumbent Disbursing Officer/Disbursing Officer to withhold any payments due the said accountable officers until their respective accountabilities are extinguished; b) Direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to desist from processing cash advances unless supported by: b.1) Copy of payroll or a list of payees with their net payments, for CAs for salaries, wages, allowances and similar payments; b.2) Estimate of expenses, for CAs for petty operating expenses; and b.3) Budget for current operating expenses for the agency field activity; c) Undertake reforms in the treasury functions, in terms of personnel and procedures, to ensure that disbursements are always based on approved vouchers, cash advances are regularly liquidated, collections are remitted daily and intact and the coffers of the government are of utmost priority; and, d) Direct the Municipal Accountant to monitor closely the timely submission of accounts from the Treasurer’s Office and to update regularly their records so that any discrepancies noted can be addressed immediately. We further recommended that a written report on the status of the foregoing accountabilities and the actions taken thereon be submitted to our office for further evaluation.
observations.managementAction: Deductions from salaries are on-going. The Clearance from Money Portia Estrellanes, an incumbent Disbursing Officer and Property Accountabilit of Miss Portia Estrellanes, an accountable officer with unliquidated cash advance and who retired from government service was not signed by the department heads.
observations.reason: The unliquidated cash advances increased in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended AAR 2016 that the Municipal Mayor: a) require the Municipal Treasurer to update her cashbooks daily, exercise adequate supervision over any personnel assigned to post the cashbooks and ensure that all cashbooks are properly safeguarded against loss or misuse; b) Direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to: b.1) account for the differences noted between the ledger and cashbook balances, and henceforth conduct a quarterly reconciliation or as often as necessary; b.2) effect actual transfer of funds in accordance with the balances in the subsidiary ledgers for the two (2) bank accounts maintained under all the three (3) funds, and henceforth ensure that a bank account is maintained under only one fund; c) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to trace the difference noted in the Cash in Bank accounts per general ledger and per schedule; d) Closely monitor the Municipal Treasurer’s compliance to the strict requirements pertaining to the handling and custody of cash and depository accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office is still on-going to retrieve the CY 2005 to 2007 records which were damaged during the onslaught of Typhoon Sendong in 2012.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of the Cash in bank account is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended AAR 2016 that Management agreed to create the Inventory Committee that will be assigned to conduct the physical count guided by the regulations prescribed under Section 490 of the GAAM, Volume I, reconcile the results thereof with the accounting records, prepare the report for submission to our office and pinpoint responsibility for missing properties. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant supply all details in the schedule of PPE additions and disposals from CYs 2014-2016, otherwise, those that cannot be accounted for shall be accordingly disallowed, and to attach the report to the annual financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office agreed to create the Inventory Committee and conduct the physical count and reconcile the results with accounting records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) The Municipal Mayor, who has overall fiscal responsibility, strictly impose the regulations regarding the grant of cash advances and, together with the Municipal Accountant, desist from approving cash advances without requiring liquidation of previously unliquidated advances in accordance with Section 89 of P.D.1445; and, b) The Municipal Accountant send demand letters and require the immediate liquidation of all cash advances and after due notice, effect the withholding of their salaries or whatever benefits due the concerned officers and employees.
observations.managementAction: Deductions from salaries are on-going. The Municipal Treasurer was granted a cash advance for payment of salaries even though the previous cash advance for payment of salaries remains unsettled.
observations.reason: A Disbursing Officer was granted a cash advance for payment of salaries even though the previous cash advance for payment of salaries remains unsettled.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LDRRM Officer be directed to ensure faithful compliance to COA Circular No. 2012-002 and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF prepared by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month as well as the LDRRMFIP duly incorporated in the AIP.
observations.managementAction: The LDRMIP was attached to the annual budget every year.
observations.reason: To date, the LDRRMO still did not submit to COA the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management strictly observe the applicable regulations on the grant of any benefits/incentives and verify against double payments to avoid similar disallowances. We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant prioritize the submission of the employment documents of the Barangay Bookkeepers and the financial records showing the sources of funds from the barangays and the corresponding payments therefrom.
observations.managementAction: The persons liable for the excess payment immediately restituted the same to the government, in accordance with Section 11.0 of DBM BC No. 2014-3. Henceforth, Management shall strictly observe the applicable regulations on the grant of any benefits/incentives and verify against double payments to avoid similar disallowances. The PEI of retiring employees was deducted from the terminal leave benefits.

observations.recommendation: We recommend AAR 2016 that management notify PNB to seek the necessary evidences/documentation to provide an accounting trail of transactions that lead to the depletion of the said balances, determine accountability of the Municipal Treasurer and other accountable officers for ensuing charges should these be borne out of negligence in the discharge of official functions, and prepare the necessary adjusting entries in the books with the optimum intent of securing the government’s rights over the said deposits, furnishing our office with a copy thereof. Henceforth, require the Municipal Accountant to regularly prepare the BRS of all existing bank accounts and monitor the opening and closure of new and old accounts.
observations.managementAction: The account was re-activated in CY 2019 and the Municipal Treasurer was requested to secure the necessary documents and to produce bank confirmations.
observations.reason: The account is subject for re-activation in CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended AAR 2014 that Management immediately effect the closure of the cash balance of the PNB COMBO accounts, and transfer the same either to LBP or DBP, whichever offers the optimal value for the LGU, in compliance with Section 311 of R.A. 7160, DOF D.O. No. 27-05, COA Circular No. 92-382, and BTr TC No. 01-2013.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer requested for the closing of the PNB combo account with PNB and the transfer of funds to LBP.
observations.reason: Sanggunian Bayan has not acted on the request of the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended AAR 2014 that: a) The Municipal Mayor closely monitor the LGU’s faithful compliance to the required submission of the quarterly trial balances and supporting reports and institute the necessary disciplinary and justifiable actions for erring officials; b) The Municipal Treasurer and Disbursing Officer submit without undue delay to the Municipal Accountant the reports of collections, disbursements and checks issued in accordance with the timelines provided above.
observations.managementAction: A written explanation was submitted and the Municipal Accounting Office instituted measures to ensure weekly submission of collection and disbursement reports and the 10th day after the end of the quarter submission of supporting schedules.
observations.reason: Most documents are still subject for validation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended AAR 2014 that management submit a written explanation for the discrepancies noted above to enable us to properly evaluate the transaction. We further recommended that the BAC, Inspectorate Team and other officers involved in the approval of the subject disbursement be made accountable for lapses in the performance of their functions, as warranted.
observations.managementAction: A written explanation was submitted. Management requested the BAC and Inspectorate Team to look into the transaction and recommended accountability for lapses.
observations.reason: Documents are still subject for validation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended AAR 2012 that management require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer, the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Assessor to submit the required documents and to secure the Transfer Certificate of Title to the lot purchased as an indispensable requirement. Also, we recommended to secure the payment of Capital Gain Tax to BIR.
observations.managementAction: The LGU appropriated funds in CY 2019 to pay the Capital Gain Tax and is pursuing the transfer/titling process.
observations.reason: To date, the LGU has not secured the Transfer Certificate of Title but funds were appropriated to pay Capital Gain Tax.

observations.recommendation: We recommended AAR 2012 that management require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor to maintain their respective property records for each real property and to prioritize the filing of application for titling of all real properties acquired by the Municipal government so that transfer certificates of title in favor of the Municipality of Jimalalud can be issued to establish the Municipality’s ownership thereto.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor to prioritize the filing of application for titling of real properties acquired by the Municipality. The Municipal Treasurer’s Office still did not maintain property cards for each of the land acquired from CY 2004 onwards.
observations.reason: Land acquired prior in the year 2004 has maintained no subsidiary ledgers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to immediately issue demand letters to the borrowers in order to collect the receivables and to designate an employee to monitor payments, to prepare demand letters, and make personal follow-up on these debtors in order that the loans will be collected.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken.
observations.reason: The documents pertaining thereto could not be located because the storeroom/ bodega collapsed due to the earthquake that struck in CY 2012.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to enroll casual employees in the GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG Fund and deduct from their wages the corresponding personal share in premiums. We further recommended that it appropriate funds for the government share in such premiums in order that these employees will be entitled to the benefits thereof.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management investigate the causes of the abnormal balances and effect the necessary correcting entries in order to fairly present the accounts in the financial statements as of December 31, 2018.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended AAR 2018 to prioritize Disaster Risk Reduction Management programs and projects and implement them as planned to ensure the availability of adequate facilities in case of disaster/calamity.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality comply with existing regulations on the hiring and assignment of work to job order personnel, otherwise it should instead create new positions that would best fit the needs of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.
observations.reason: Personal Services limitation hindered the LGU from hiring new employees to perform clerical works.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, spearheaded by the MENRO Designate and MPDC, fast-track the preparation of the required plans under the disaster preparedness component to provide effective guidance in the implementation of programs outlined therein, and fully undertake measures to comply with the requirements to pass the assessment which includes the sanitary landfill in order to gain access to the PCF and the incentives package which could provide essential funding for the LGU’s programs in addressing the eventuality of a disaster.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.
observations.reason: The LGU failed to construct a materials recovery facility, a requirement to qualify for the Seal of Good Local Governance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) The Municipal Mayor closely monitor the LGU’s faithful compliance to the required submission of the accounts and reports and institute the necessary disciplinary and justifiable actions for erring officials; b) The Municipal Treasurer and respective Disbursing Officer submit without undue delay to the Municipal Accountant the reports of collections, disbursements and checks issued in accordance with the timelines provided above.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.
observations.reason: The Disbursing Officer and the Municipal Treasurer still failed to submit the Report of Disbursements, Report of Checks Issued and Report of Collections to the Municipal Accountant within the reglementary periods. Neither did the Municipal Mayor monitor compliance by these personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management notify the concerned individuals or entities to settle their accounts or initiate appropriate actions to recover the amounts and to request authority from the Commission on Audit through the Sangguniang Bayan to write off the amounts determined to be no longer valid.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant maintained subsidiary ledgers for real property and the Municipal Assessor prioritized applications for titling of properties acquired onwards.
observations.reason: Land acquired prior to the year 2004 has no subsidiary ledgers thus they could not identify and show the details of the said land.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor fill up the mandatory position of Municipal Accountant with a person possessing the appropriate qualifications or advertise the vacancy and give the widest publicity in order to attract qualified applicants. We further recommended that the vacancy be advertised and given the widest publicity in order to attract qualified applicants.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 The Mayor hired a Certified Public Accountant but did not designate him as Municipal Accountant. He was given the position of Clerk II.
observations.reason: Although a CPA was hired he only hold the positon of Clerk II.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality comply with existing regulations on the hiring and assignment of work to job order personnel, otherwise it should instead create new positions that would best fit the needs of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 No action.
observations.reason: Personal Services limitation hindered the LGU from hiring new employees to perform clerical works.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management notify the concerned individuals or entities to settle their accounts or initiate appropriate actions to recover the amounts and to request authority from the Commission on Audit through the Sanguniang Bayan to write off the amounts determined to be no longer valid.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 No action was taken by Management.
observations.reason: No records on hand to identify the individuals who were granted cash advances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the OIC-Municipal Accountant agreed to take up the
entry to recognize the assets in the General Fund books as follows:

Account Title Account Code Debit Credit
Breeding Stocks 1-08-01-010 966,800.00
Road Networks 1-07-03-010 6,664,800.00
Government Equity 3-01-01 7,631,184.00

We recommended and Management agreed to immediately issue demand
letters to the borrowers in order to collect the receivables and to thereafter designate
an employee to monitor payments, to prepare demand letters, and make personal
follow-up on these debtors in order that the loans will be collected.

We recommended and Management agreed to enroll casual employees in the
GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG Fund and deduct from their wages the
corresponding personal share in premiums. We further recommended that it
appropriate funds for the government share in such premiums in order that these
employees will be entitled to the benefits thereof.

We recommended and management agreed to investigate the causes of the
abnormal balances and effect the necessary correcting entries in order to fairly
present the accounts in the financial statements as of December 31, 2018.

We recommended and Management agreed to consider implementing the
projects by contract instead of by administration considering the lack of technical
personnel to supervise project implementation, or to hire additional engineering
personnel to assist the Municipal Engineer in the implementation thereof.

We recommended and the Inspector agreed to see to it that all conditions
stated in the purchase order are strictly adhered to before accepting the delivery.
We also recommended that the Municipality ensure that no blanks are left in the
Acceptance and Inspection Report, for better control and monitoring.

We recommended and Management agreed to prioritize Disaster Risk
Reduction Management programs and projects and implement them as planned to
ensure the availability of adequate facilities in case of disaster/calamity.

We recommended and the Municipal Social Welfare and Development
Officer and the Budget Officer agreed to ensure that only those
projects/programs/activities which clearly address gender issues are allocated from
GAD appropriations in order to ensure the attainment of GAD goals.

We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Accountant find all means to retrieve the records pertaining to the sources of the properties in order that assets recorded in the trust fund can be totally transferred to the proper accounts in the general fund for proper presentation of the financial statements. Also, we recommended that the Offices of the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer maintain property, plant and equipment ledger cards and property cards, respectively, in accordance with existing regulations. Lastly, we recommended that the Municipality hire additional accounting personnel to augment the present manpower.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 Journal entry 2017 was made per JEV No. 100-2018-12-4824.
observations.reason: The Accounting Office still did not maintain property/property, plant and Equiment ledger cards.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant take up the necessary adjusting journal entries. Further, we recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the bank reconciliation statements within ten (10) days from receipt after the end of each month as provided under Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 so that the validity and existence of the cash in bank could be ascertained.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 The OIC-Municipal Accountant submitted bank reconciliation statements up to December 31, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that claims are duly supported with all the required documents in accordance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445, Sections 5.13, 9.1.3.1 and 13.1 COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Sections 2, 3 and 4 of COA Circular Nos. 77-61 in order to ensure the validity, propriety and legality thereof.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 Transactions were duly supported with all the required documents in accordance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445, Sections 5.13, 9.1.3.1 and 13.1 COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Sections 2, 3 and 4 of COA Circular Nos. 77-61.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan institute internal rules to regulate, reduce and limit the grant of phone/load allowance to the Mayor, Vice-Mayor and department heads pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-003. We further recommended subscription to mobile phone plans instead of offering unlimited talk and text for very low prices to preclude the incurrence of excessive mobile phone expenses.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 The Municipal Mayor instituted internal rules limiting cellphone load allowance for officials/department heads to ₱2,000.00 per month.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist recall all the substandard plows and return these to the supplier for replacement with good-quality plows or a refund. We further recommended that the BAC/TWG invariably conduct post-qualification by inspecting and testing the goods/products procured in order to ensure that the items delivered are of standard quality in order to avoid disadvantage to the government.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 The Municipal Agriculturist recalled or repaired the plows and returned/ repaired the plows at the expense of the supplier.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the barangay appropriate funds for the repair of the cracks to avoid further deterioration of the concrete roads. We also recommended the backfill of the road shoulders, construction of drainage canals, clearing of vegetation along the road and installation of steel barriers where required, to protect motorists from falling into the ravines. Lastly, we recommended that the LGU exert extra efforts to hasten the release of additional funds from BFAR in order to complete the wharf.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 The barangay appropriated funds for the maintenance of the canals. The construction of wharf was completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) direct the Local Finance Committee to study and explore possibilities of increasing local revenues in order to minimize the municipality’s dependence on the IRA to finance its operations; b) direct the Municipal Treasurer to verify from the records of the BIR the accuracy of the declared gross receipts of the taxpayers in order to assess the correct amount of tax. Further, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer thoroughly examine the books of accounts of business taxpayers and conduct confirmation of the declared gross receipts.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 The LGU passed an amended tax code.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) Order the incumbent Paymaster/Disbursing Officer to withhold any payments due the said accountable officers until their respective accountabilities are extinguished; b) Direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to desist from processing cash advances unless supported by: b.1) Copy of the payroll or a list of payees with their net payments, for CAs for salaries, wages, allowances and similar payments; b.2) Estimate of expenses, for CAs for petty operating expenses; and b.3) Budget for current operating expenses for the agency field activity; c) Undertake reforms in the treasury functions, in terms of personnel and procedures, to ensure that disbursements are always based on approved vouchers, cash advances are regularly liquidated, collections are remitted daily and intact and the coffers of the government are of utmost priority; and, d) Direct the Municipal Accountant to monitor closely the timely submission of accounts from the Treasurer’s Office and to update regularly their records so that any discrepancies noted can be addressed immediately. We further recommended that a written report on the status of the foregoing accountabilities and the actions taken thereon be submitted to our office for further evaluation.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 Deductions from the salaries are on-going. The Municipal Treasurer requested for the passage of a resolution requesting to withhold payments.
observations.reason: The accountable officers have not fully paid their cash accountability to date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LPRAT, through the BUB Focal Person, intensify efforts to fast-track the implementation of the BUB program by assigning technical personnel who will focus on BUB projects and by closely monitoring thereon for the LGU to achieve its target of reducing poverty and likewise avert any repercussions that may result from its low implementation rate. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for all trust receipts, complete with details and reconciled with controlling accounts, in order to facilitate reporting on actual fund utilization.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 The LGU was able to fully implement port and wharf projects Phases 1 and 2.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, spearheaded by the MENRO Designate and MPDC, fast-track the preparation of the required plans under the disaster preparedness component to qualify for the Seal of Good Local Governance and fully undertake measures to comply with the requirements to pass the assessment which would include the sanitary landfill in order to gain access to the PCF and the incentives package which could provide essential funding for the LGU’s programs in addressing the eventuality of a disaster.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 The LGU has prioritized the preparation of the required plans and conducted initial measures to comply with the requirements to pass the assessment which would include the sanitary landfill requirement.
observations.reason: The LGU operated a controlled open dump site and was not able to construct the materials recovery facility, a requirement to qualify for the Seal of Good Local Governance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) Require the Municipal Treasurer to update her cashbooks daily, reconciling their respective records. b) Direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to: b.1) account for the differences noted between the ledger and cashbook balances, and henceforth conduct a quarterly reconciliation or as often as necessary; b.2) effect actual transfer of funds in accordance with the balances in the subsidiary ledgers for the two (2) bank accounts maintained under all the three (3) funds, and henceforth ensure that a bank account is maintained under only one fund; c) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to trace the difference noted in the Cash in Bank accounts per general ledger and per schedule; d) Closely monitor the Municipal Treasurer’s compliance to the strict requirements pertaining to the handling and custody of cash and depository accounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 The reconciliation of Cash in Bank is still on-going. The Municipal Treasurer’s and Municipal Accountant’s Offices are still updating their records.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of Cash in Bank is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the budget for the LCPC be allocated in the annual budget as a separate appropriation computed at not less than 1% of the IRA. We further recommended that the Council prioritize the formulation of the LDPC, LIPC and the LSCR and ensure that the plans and budget are adequately funded, utilized and reported on through the Accomplishment Report in order to provide monitoring measures for the attainment of the program’s objectives.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 The LGU allocated 1% of the IRA for the LCPC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to identify, inventory and classify its insurable properties, submit the report to the GSIS not later than October 31 of each year, and appropriate the necessary premiums in its annual budget under the General Fund or the 5% LDRRMF, and insure the same with the GIF under the GSIS every year.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 Not applicable because RA No. 656 requires only first class municipalities, cities and provinces to appropriate the premiums and insure their properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to create the Inventory Committee that will be assigned to conduct the physical count guided by the regulations prescribed under Section 490 of the GAAM, Volume I, reconcile the results thereof with the accounting records, prepare the report for submission to our office and pinpoint responsibility for missing properties. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant supply all the details in the schedule of PPE additions and disposals from CYs 2014-2016, otherwise, those that cannot be accounted for shall be accordingly disallowed, and to attach the report to the annual financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 The department heads prepared listings of property, plant and equipment inventory seen in their respective LGU offices. The Municipal Engineer conducted an inventory of Motor Vehicles.
observations.reason: Lack of adequate knowledge in conducting physical inventory hindered the implementing offices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor identify, inventory and classify the high-value items and effect the necessary accounting entries to recognize these as PPE in accordance with PPSAS 17; require the Property Custodian/Officer to prepare an ARE for the PPE recognized; and require officials involved in the approval of the DV to refund the overpayment immediately. Henceforth, ensure strict compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and other pertinent regulations.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Overpayment amounting to ₱22,878.00 was fully paid on 10/12/17 per OR No. 1898752.
observations.reason: The Management still did not submit documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that: a) The Municipal Mayor, who has overall fiscal responsibility, strictly impose the regulations regarding the grant of cash advances and, together with the Municipal Accountant, desist from approving cash advances without requiring liquidation of previously unliquidated advances in accordance with Section 89 of P.D. 1445; and, b) The Municipal Accountant send demand letters and require the immediate liquidation of all cash advances and after due notice, effect the withholding of their salaries or whatever benefits due the concerned officers and employees.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 The LGU strictly barred officials and employees from availing of the new cash advances unless previous cash advances are settled. Deductions from salaries were implemented.
observations.reason: Employees not in service and residing outside the Philippines failed to refund previous cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LDRRM Officer be directed to ensure faithful compliance to COA Circular No. 2012-002 and regularly submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF prepared by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month as well as the LDRRMFIP duly incorporated in the AIP.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 The Report of Utilization was regularly submitted to the LDRRMF Officer and the Municipal Accountant prepared and submitted the Report of Utilization.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel to assist the LDRRMF Officer in preparing/submitting the Report of Utilization to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management discontinue deducting from the payroll the repayments due to private financing institutions, except for loans availed by the employees prior to the issuance of this Audit Observation Memorandum or until such loans shall have been fully paid and, henceforth, desist from entering into similar contracts/Memorandum of Agreement with financing institutions not otherwise authorized under the GAA.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 The LGU stopped deducting loan repayments due to private institutions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly implement the required minimum net take home pay of Three Thousand Pesos (₱3,000.00) mandated under Section 48 of R.A. 10651. Direct the concerned officials involved in the processing of loans to ensure that this provision of law is invariably enforced and to register their disapproval of loan applications if warranted by the circumstances.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 The LGU sees to it that take home pay is not below the minimum amount set by the General Appropriations Act. Directives were issued to concerned officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the persons liable for the excess payment of the CY 2014 PEI immediately restitute the same to the government, in accordance with Section 11.0 of DBM BC No. 2014-3. Henceforth, Management shall strictly observe the applicable regulations on the grant of any leave benefits/incentives and verify against double payments to avoid similar disallowances. We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant prioritize the submission of the employment documents of the Barangay Bookkeepers and the financial records showing the sources of funds from the barangays and the corresponding payments therefrom.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 The officials and persons liable for the excess payment were required to restitute the PEI immediately. Deductions from terminal benefits of retiring employees were implemented. Some employees paid in installment basis.
observations.reason: Not all employees were able to pay in full.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to make representations with PNB to seek the necessary evidences/documentation to provide an accounting trail of transactions that lead to the depletion of the said balances including the abnormal decrease in the time deposit account, determine accountability of the Municipal Treasurer and other accountable officers for the ensuing charges should these be borne out of negligence in the discharge of official functions, and prepare the necessary adjusting entries in the books with the optimum intent of securing the government’s rights over the said deposits, furnishing our office with a copy thereof. Henceforth, require the Municipal Accountant to regularly prepare the BRS of all existing bank accounts and monitor the opening and closure of new and old accounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 The Municipal Treasurer personally talked with the PNB manager to provide an accounting trail of the transaction.
observations.reason: The bank cannot provide an accounting trail of the transaction.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality immediately effect the closure of the PNB Time Deposit and COMBO accounts, and transfer the same either to LBP or DBP, whichever offers the optimal value for the LGU, in compliance with Section 311 of R.A. 7160, DOF D.O. No. 27-05, COA Circular No. 92-382, and BTr TC No. 01-2013.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 The Municipal Treasurer requested for the passage of a resolution to close the PNB Time Deposits.
observations.reason: To date, the Sangguniang Bayan has not acted on the request for the closure of the PNB Time deposits.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management thru the Municipal Solid Waste Mgt. Board: a) Concretize methods and procedures for the phase-out and eventual closure of existing open and/or controlled dumps within a specific time frame; b) Request the members of the Sangguniang Bayan to prioritize the allocation of funds for the establishment and operation of a sanitary landfill in accordance with Sections 40 and 41 of R.A. 9003; and c) Advocate for a full-time MENRO with its own office and staff to focus and spearhead the implementation of the approved Integrated Solid Waste Management Plan which was painstakingly formulated.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 The LGU appropriated ₱1,500,000.00 in CY 2019 as site for the sanitary landfill and actions were initiated for phase-out.
observations.reason: The LGU may not find a suitable area for a sanitary landfill.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a) closely monitor the LGU’s faithful compliance to the required submission of accounts and institute the necessary disciplinary and justifiable actions for erring officials; and b) that the Municipal Treasurer and Disbursing Officer submit without undue delay to the Municipal Accountant the reports of collections, disbursements and checks issued in accordance with the timelines provided above.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 The submission of the required accounts and reports were deferred because of the lack of space to store the vouchers, it did not include report of collections and disbursements.
observations.reason: Although the vouchers were held in abeyance due to lack of space, the LGU did not include the accounts and reports of collections and disbursements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit a written explanation for the discrepancies noted to enable us to properly evaluate the transaction. We further recommend that the BAC, Inspectorate Team and other officers involved in the approval of the subject disbursement be made accountable for lapses in the performance of their functions, as warranted.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 A written explanation was submitted to the previous team in CY 2015.
observations.reason: The documents are still subject to validation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to dispose of the unserviceable properties in accordance with the disposal provisions of COA Circular 92-386.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 The unserviceable assets were transferred to the Other Property, Plant and Equipment account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management notify the concerned individuals or entities to settle their accounts or initiate appropriate actions to recover the amounts and to request authority from the Commission on Audit through the Sanguniang Bayan to write off the amounts determined to be no longer valid. Based on the certified list of tax collectibles for the year, require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to set up the Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable in the General Fund and Special Education Fund, respectively, at the beginning of the year. This procedure should be applied retroactively, starting CY 2002 when the NGAS took effect.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 The Municipal Treasurer furnished the Municipal Assessor the certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year.
observations.reason: Not all supporting documents were furnished by the Municipal Assessor to prepare the List of Real Property Taxpayers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer, the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Assessor to submit the required documents and to secure the Transfer Certificate of Title to the lot purchased as an indispensable requirement.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 The LGU processed the necessary documents for the issuance of the new title.
observations.reason: Awaiting issuance of new title.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor to maintain their respective property records for each real property and the Municipal Assessor to prioritize the filing of application for titling of all real properties acquired by the Municipal government so that transfer certificates of title in favor of the Municipality of Jimalalud can be issued to establish the Municipality’s ownership thereto.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 The Municipal Assessor maintained subsidiary ledgers and prioritized the application for titling of properties.
observations.reason: Subsidiary ledgers maintained by the Municipal Accountant did not show details of the land account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to enroll casual employees in the GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG Fund and have them subjected to the personal share of GSIS life and retirement deductions, in compliance with the provision of Section 2.1.1 of RA 8291 or the GSIS Act of 1997.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 The LGU covered only 5 casuals as GSIS members. Several casual employees are still not GSIS members.
observations.reason: Out of the 20 casuals only five casual were GSIS members.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management notify the concerned individuals or entities to settle their accounts or initiate appropriate actions to recover the amounts and to request authority from the Commission on Audit through the Sanguniang Bayan to write off the amounts determined to be no longer valid.
observations.managementAction: No Action was taken by the LGU.
observations.reason: The concerned individuals or entities could no longer be located.

observations.recommendation:
RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Accountant find all means
to retrieve the records pertaining to the sources of the properties in order that assets
recorded in the trust fund can be totally transferred to the proper accounts in the
general fund for proper presentation of the financial statements pursuant to Section
104 of the Manual on NGAS. Also, we recommended that the Offices of the
Municipal Accountant and Treasurer maintain property, plant and equipment
ledger cards and property cards, respectively, in accordance with existing
regulations. Lastly, we recommended that the Municipality hire additional
accounting personnel to augment the present manpower.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant take up the necessary
adjusting journal entries (please see Annex 15).

Further, we recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant prepare and
submit the bank reconciliation statements within ten (10) days from receipt after the
end of each month as provided under Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated
October 2, 1996 so that the validity and existence of the cash in bank could be
ascertained.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that claims are duly
supported with all the required supporting documents in accordance with Section
4(6) of PD 1445, Sections 5.13, 9.1.3.1 and 13.1 COA Circular No. 2012-001 and
Sections 2, 3 and 4 of COA Circular Nos. 77-61 in order to ensure the validity,
propriety and legality thereof.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan institute internal rules to
regulate, reduce and limit the grant of phone/load allowance to the Mayor, Vice-
Mayor and department heads pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-003. We further
recommended subscription to mobile phone plans offering unlimited talk and text
for very low prices to preclude the incurrence of excessive mobile phone expenses.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist recall all the substandard
plows and return these to the supplier for replacement with good-quality plows or a
refund. We further recommended that the BAC/TWG invariably conduct post-
qualification by inspecting and testing the goods/products procured in order to
ensure that the items delivered are of standard quality in order to avoid
disadvantage to the government.

RECOMMENDATIONS:
We recommended that the LGU appropriate funds for the repair of the
cracks to avoid further deterioration of the concrete roads. We also recommended
the backfill of the road shoulders, construction of drainage canals, clearing of
vegetation along the road and installation of steel barriers where required, to
protect motorists from falling into the ravines. Lastly, we recommended that the
LGU exert extra efforts to hasten the release of additional funds from BFAR in
order to complete the wharf.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor fill up the mandatory position
of Municipal Accountant with a person possessing the appropriate qualifications
and eligibility pursuant to Section 474 of RA 7160. We further recommended that
the vacancy be advertised and given the widest publicity in order to attract qualified
applicants.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor a) direct the Local Finance
Committee to study and explore possibilities of increasing local revenues in order to
minimize the municipality’s dependence on the IRA to finance its operations; b)
direct the Municipal Treasurer to verify from the records of the BIR the accuracy of
the declared gross receipts of the taxpayers in order to assess the correct amount of
tax.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended that the Municipality comply with existing regulations on
the hiring and assignment of work to job order personnel, otherwise it should
instead create new positions that would best fit the needs of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to recognize the inventory/PPE and income/expense accounts, revise the Statement of Financial Performance and Statement of Financial Position, review the CY 2015 transactions for similar omissions. We further recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Accountant ensure strict compliance to the PPSAS in order to fairly present the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: 2016 The Municipal AAR Accountant revised the financial statements in accordance with the PPSAS

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review all financial records and reports in order to ascertain the validity of the account balances that should be presented in the financial statements and to ensure that reports are prepared invariably in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles and Section 69 of COA Circular No. 2004-008.
observations.managementAction: 2016 The Accounting AAR Office corrected the errors.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) Order the incumbent Paymaster/Disbursing Officer to withhold any payments due the said accountable officers until their respective refunding accountabilities are extinguished; b) Direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to desist from processing cash advances unless supported by: b.1) Copy of the payroll or a list of payees with their net payments, for CAs for salaries, wages, allowances and similar payments; b.2) Estimate of expenses, for CAs for petty operating expenses; and b.3) Budget for current operating expenses for the agency field activity; c) Undertake reforms in the treasury functions, in terms of personnel and procedures, to ensure that disbursements are always based on approved vouchers, cash advances are regularly liquidated, collections are remitted daily and intact and the coffers of the government are of utmost priority; and, d) Direct the Municipal Accountant to monitor closely the timely submission of accounts from the Treasurer’s Office and to update regularly their records so that any discrepancies noted can be addressed immediately.
We further recommended that a written report on the status of the foregoing accountabilities and the actions taken thereon be submitted to our office for further evaluation.
observations.managementAction: 2016 AAR The four (4) former Disbursing Officers were monthly payroll deductions.
observations.reason: To date the amount of refund amounted only to P 147,179.27.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LPRAT, through the BUB Focal Person, intensify efforts to fast-track the implementation of the BUB program by assigning technical personnel who will focus on BUB projects and by closely monitoring progress thereon for the LGU to achieve its target of reducing poverty and likewise avert any repercussions that may result from its low implementation rate. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for all trust receipts, complete with details and reconciled with controlling accounts, in order to facilitate reporting on actual fund utilization.
observations.managementAction: 2016 The LGU was able to fully implement port and wharf Phase 1 and 2, due to road right of way problem of Barangay Eli was not implemented while the marine reserves sanctuary and farm to market roads at Mahanlud and Salintubig, two protective services projects. The Municipal Accountant now prepares subsidiary ledgers for all moneys received as trust funds.
observations.reason: Not implemented due to increase in prices of construction materials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management explore other alternative modes to accelerate the rate of implementation of the LGSF-funded infrastructure projects and augment technical personnel, where necessary, and thus shorten the length of time that funds are left idle.
observations.managementAction: The LGU fully implemented the seven projects

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, spearheaded by the MENRO Designate and MPDC, fast-track the preparation of the required plans under the disaster preparedness component to provide effective guidance in the implementation of programs outlined therein, and fully undertake measures to comply with the requirements to pass the SGLG assessment which includes the sanitary landfill in order to gain access to the PCF and the incentives package which could provide essential funding for the LGU’s programs in addressing the eventuality of a disaster.
observations.managementAction: The LGU now operates a controlled dump site
observations.reason: The LGU failed to construct a sanitary materials recovery facility, hence not qualify for the Seal of Good Local Governance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor fill up the mandatory position of Municipal Accountant with a person possessing the appropriate qualifications and eligibility pursuant to Section 474 of RA 7160. We further recommended that the vacancy be advertised and given the widest publicity in order to attract qualified applicants.
observations.managementAction: The Human Resources and Management Officer told us that the Civil Service Commission, Regional Office No. VII, is going to issue a Resolution on the matter within a week.
observations.reason: The case has not been resolved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor a) direct the Local Finance Committee to study and explore possibilities of increasing local revenues in order to minimize the municipality’s dependence on the IRA to finance its operations; b) direct the Municipal Treasurer to verify from the records of the BIR the accuracy of the declared gross receipts of the taxpayers in order to assess the correct amount of tax.
observations.managementAction: During the exit conference the Municipal Treasurer showed to the Audit Team Official Receipt Nos. 2014642 and 2014643 both dated June 20, 2018 as additional payment of the business tax due of WCT Construction and Enterprise and JRM Commercial and General Merchandise in CY 2017 amounting to 26,447.76 and 27,719.93. She also informed us that the Revised Tax Code of the Municipality was submitted to the Sanggunian Bayan of Jimalalud last May 22, 2018, which is currently under the second reading. Lastly, she promised to see to it that Income Statements submitted by the Taxpayers have been duly received by the BIR for proper assessment. However, if the taxpayers fail to submit the Income Statement, she would recommend to the Municipal Mayor the issuance of Temporary business permits which would last for 3 months.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality comply with existing regulations on the hiring and assignment of work to job order personnel, otherwise it should instead create new positions that would best fit the needs of the LGU.
