observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the necessary
adjusting entries to recognize the inventory/PPE and income/expense accounts, revise
the Statement of Financial Performance and Statement of Financial Position, and
review the CY 2015 transactions for similar omissions. We further recommended that,
henceforth, the Municipal Accountant ensure strict compliance to the PPSAS in order
to fairly present the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to review all financial records
and reports in order to ascertain the validity of the account balances that should be
presented in the financial statements and to ensure that reports are prepared invariably
in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles and Section 69 of COA
Circular No. 2004-008.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Order the incumbent Paymaster/Disbursing Officer to withhold any payments due
the said accountable officers until their respective accountabilities are
extinguished;
b) Direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to desist from processing cash
advances unless supported by:
b.1) Copy of the payroll or a list of payees with their net payments, for CAs for
salaries, wages, allowances and similar payments;
b.2) Estimate of expenses, for CAs for petty operating expenses; and
b.3) Budget for current operating expenses for the agency field activity;
c) Undertake reforms in the treasury functions, in terms of personnel and procedures,
to ensure that disbursements are always based on approved vouchers, cash
advances are regularly liquidated, collections are remitted daily and intact and the
coffers of the government are of utmost priority; and,
d) Direct the Municipal Accountant to monitor closely the timely submission of
accounts from the Treasurer’s Office and to update regularly their records so that
any discrepancies noted can be addressed immediately.

We further recommended that a written report on the status of the foregoing
accountabilities and the actions taken thereon be submitted to our office for further
evaluation.

We recommended that the LPRAT, through the BUB Focal Person, intensify efforts to
fast-track the implementation of the BUB program by assigning technical personnel
who will focus on BUB projects and by closely monitoring progress thereon for the
LGU to achieve its target of reducing poverty and likewise avert any repercussions that
may result from its low implementation rate. We further recommended that the
Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for all trust receipts, complete with
details and reconciled with controlling accounts, in order to facilitate reporting on
actual fund utilization.

We recommended that Management explore other alternative modes to accelerate the
rate of implementation of the LGSF-funded infrastructure projects and augment
technical personnel, where necessary, and thus shorten the length of time that funds
are left idle.

We recommended that Management, spearheaded by the MENRO Designate and
MPDC, fast-track the preparation of the required plans under the disaster
preparedness component to provide effective guidance in the implementation of
programs outlined therein, and fully undertake measures to comply with the
requirements to pass the SGLG assessment which includes the sanitary landfill in
order to gain access to the PCF and the incentives package which could provide
essential funding for the LGU’s programs in addressing the eventuality of a disaster.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Require the Municipal Treasurer to update her cashbooks daily, exercise adequate
supervision over any personnel assigned to post the transactions, and ensure that
all cashbooks are properly safeguarded against loss or misuse;
b) Direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to:
b.1) account for the differences noted between the ledger and cashbook balances,
and henceforth conduct a quarterly reconciliation or as often as necessary;
b.2) effect actual transfer of funds in accordance with the balances in the
subsidiary ledgers for the two (2) bank accounts maintained under all the
three (3) funds, and henceforth ensure that a bank account is maintained
under only one fund;
c) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to trace the difference noted in the Cash in
Bank accounts per general ledger and per schedule;
d) Closely monitor the Municipal Treasurer’s compliance to the strict requirements
pertaining to the handling and custody of cash and depository accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed that the budget for the LCPC be allocated
in the annual budget as a separate appropriation computed at not less than 1% of the
IRA. We further recommended that the Council prioritize the formulation of the
LDPC, LIPC and the LSCR and ensure that the plans and budget are adequately
funded, utilized and reported on through the Accomplishment Report in order to
provide monitoring measures for the attainment of the program’s objectives.

We recommended and Management agreed to identify, inventory and classify its
insurable properties, submit the report to the GSIS not later than October 31 of each
year, appropriate the necessary premiums in its annual budget under the General
Fund or the 5% LDRRMF, and insure the same with the GIF under the GSIS every
year.

We recommended and Management agreed to create the Inventory Committee that will
be assigned to conduct the physical count guided by the regulations prescribed under
Section 490 of the GAAM, Volume I, reconcile the results thereof with the accounting
records, prepare the report for submission to our office and pinpoint responsibility for
missing properties. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant supply
all the details in the schedule of PPE additions and disposals from CYs 2014-2016,
otherwise, those that cannot be accounted for shall be accordingly disallowed, and to
attach the report to the annual financial statements.

We recommend that Management submit within the period stipulated the necessary documents enumerated in the NS to be issued. We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare an adjusting entry recognizing the high-value items as PPE in accordance with PPSAS 17. We likewise recommend that the Property Custodian/Officer prepare an ARE for the PPE recognized. Lastly, we recommend that the officials involved in the approval of the DV immediately the overpayment of P22,278.00. Henceforth, ensure strict compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No, 2012-001 and other pertinent regulations.

We further recommend that Management comply with the requirements/documents contained in the Notice of Suspension to be issued and to submit a written explanation why the bidding had to take place in the ensuing year CY 2015 when the PR and notices to bidders were prepared in CY 2014 despite the urgency of the items requested.

observations.managementAction: The documents were submitted to ROD and BIR. Transfer of Certificate of Title is (almost done).
observations.reason: The Transfer of Certificate of Title is still in process.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant-Designate to maintain their respective property records for each real property and the Municipal Assessor to prioritize the filing of application for titling of all real properties acquired by the Municipal government so that transfer certificates of title in favor of the Municipality of Jimalalud can be issued to establish the Municipality’s ownership thereto.
observations.managementAction: Some purchased lots already have Certificate of Title in the name of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Appropriate amounts intended for casual employees GSIS life and retirement premiums and have them subjected to the personal share of GSIS life and retirement deductions, in compliance with the provision of Section 2.1.1 of RA 8291 or the GSIS Act of 1997.
observations.managementAction: Only 5 employees are covered by GSIS.
observations.reason: Funds from the Gen. Fund are not enough to sustain its full implementation. Plus, actual status of their employment, whether they are valid casual employees or purely job order personnel, still has to be established.
